symptoms.symptom_name: Deep, gaping wound with visible subcutaneous fat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Defines the need for surgical closure rather than simple dressing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bleeding partially controlled by direct pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Response to direct pressure suggests a venous rather than arterial source.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal motor and sensory function distally

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved function makes major tendon/nerve injury less likely, though formal exam is still needed.
Overdue booster status relevant to wound management.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact distal pulses, capillary refill, and full motor/sensory function on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A deep, gaping wound with controlled venous bleeding and preserved distal function together indicate a stable but clinically significant laceration needing prompt in-person closure.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Prompt in-person wound irrigation, exploration, and suturing (GP with procedural skills or ED)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Optimises healing, cosmesis, and infection prevention within the golden period.
Booster overdue given more than 10 years since last dose.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home bandaging alone without formal wound closure
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would risk poor healing, increased infection risk, and worse scarring for a wound of this depth.
